title: camassia leichtlinii subsp. suksdorfii
intro: Camassia leichtlinii subsp. suksdorfii, also known as Great Camas, is a perennial plant native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. This plant contains inulin, a type of prebiotic fiber, which is said to have medicinal properties, including the ability to lower blood sugar levels and support gut health. The health benefits of Camassia leichtlinii subsp. suksdorfii include its potential to aid in weight management, improve digestion, and even exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making a tea from the roots or leaves, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
